title: Tangy Dense Slovakian Turkey Ham Dumplings
description: A comforting Slovakian-inspired dish featuring hearty turkey ham dumplings simmered in a tangy tomato-broth, perfect for a cozy date night.
instructions: In a large bowl, combine minced turkey ham, flour blend, salt, pepper, and egg. Stir until well mixed. Add almond milk gradually, mixing until a firm but pliable dough forms. Let rest 10 minutes.
While dough rests, prepare sauce: In a saucepan, sauté onion and garlic in a splash of oil until translucent. Add tomato paste and cook 2 minutes.
Pour in vegetable broth, bring to a gentle simmer, then add apple cider vinegar. Season with salt and pepper, simmer 5 minutes to meld flavors.
Take spoonfuls of dough (about 1 tablespoon) and shape into small dumplings. Gently drop them into the simmering sauce. Do not overcrowd; cook in batches.
Cover and cook dumplings for 10 minutes, or until they rise and the dough is cooked through.
Stir in chopped parsley just before serving, allowing it to wilt slightly.
Taste the sauce; adjust acidity with more vinegar or seasoning as needed.
Transfer dumplings to a serving dish, spoon some sauce over them.
Serve hot, optionally with a side of steamed greens or a fresh green salad.
